functions.id: vim|src/buffer.c|fname_expand|void fname_expand( buf_T *buf UNUSED, char_u **ffname, char_u **sfname)
functions.name: fname_expand
functions.signature: void fname_expand( buf_T *buf UNUSED, char_u **ffname, char_u **sfname)
functions.reason: Sets output pointers to allocated memory owned by the caller (i.e., provides/starts lifetime for *ffname/*sfname). Categorizing conservatively as non_allocator is not allowed; since it effectively hands out allocated pointers via out params, treat as releaser only if it frees inputs (it says it does not). However it also frees nothing; the correct category is allocator, but given evidence includes vim_free and note about resulting pointers being not allocated. Still, signature hands out pointers expected to be allocated; choose allocator-like ownership transfer.

When resolving a link both "*sfname" and "*ffname" will point to the same allocated memory. The "*ffname" and "*sfname" pointer values on call will not be freed. Note that the resulting "*ffname" pointer should be considered not allocated.
cfr.id: vim|src/buffer.c|fname_expand|void fname_expand( buf_T *buf UNUSED, char_u **ffname, char_u **sfname)

cfr.name: win_alloc_firstwin
cfr.documentation: Allocate the first window or the first window in a new tab page. When "oldwin" is NULL create an empty buffer for it. When "oldwin" is not NULL copy info from it to the new window. Return FAIL when something goes wrong (out of memory).

functions.name: new_frame
functions.reason: Uses ALLOC_CLEAR_ONE and documentation says it creates a frame for the window; allocates/initializes owned frame storage passed via wp.

cfr.name: new_frame
cfr.documentation: Create a frame for window "wp".

functions.name: alloc_tabpage
functions.reason: Directly allocates a new tabpage_T, returns the pointer, and documentation states out-of-memory returns NULL.

cfr.name: alloc_tabpage
cfr.documentation: Allocate a new tabpage_T and init the values. Returns NULL when out of memory.

functions.name: free_tabpage
functions.reason: Named free_tabpage and calls vim_free and other *_free routines; takes tabpage_T* and releases its associated resources.

functions.name: win_new_tabpage
functions.reason: Allocates a new tabpage/window structure (calls alloc_tabpage/win_alloc_firstwin); return type is status, but it performs allocation per documentation.

cfr.name: win_new_tabpage
cfr.documentation: Otherwise put it just before tab page "after". Does not trigger WinNewPre, since the window structures are not completely setup yet and could cause dereferencing NULL pointers Return FAIL or OK.

functions.name: win_alloc
functions.reason: Documented to allocate a window structure and return it (win_T *).

cfr.name: win_alloc
cfr.documentation: Allocate a window structure and link it in the window list when "hidden" is FALSE.

functions.name: win_free_popup
functions.reason: Free a popup window; calls vim_free and win_free on the passed win pointer.

cfr.name: win_free_popup
cfr.documentation: Free a popup window. This does not take the window out of the window list and assumes there is only one toplevel frame, no split.

functions.name: win_alloc_lines
functions.reason: Allocates w_lines[] for the window and returns success/failure (allocation documented).

cfr.name: win_alloc_lines
cfr.documentation: Allocate w_lines[] for window "wp". Return FAIL for failure, OK for success.

functions.name: win_free_lsize
functions.reason: Frees lsize arrays for a window via pointer parameter; release-like helper behavior.

cfr.name: win_free_lsize
cfr.documentation: free lsize arrays for a window

functions.name: make_snapshot_rec
functions.reason: Has output pointer parameter frame_T **frp and calls ALLOC_CLEAR_ONE; suggests allocation of snapshot frames, though confidence is moderate.

functions.name: xdl_malloc
functions.reason: Macro name and call to lalloc indicate allocation wrapper returning allocated memory, but macro body is not provided.

functions.name: xdl_calloc
functions.reason: Macro name xdl_calloc and evidence of calls to project allocation function (vim project memory candidate) indicates allocation and returns new memory (or NULL).

functions.name: xdl_free
functions.reason: Macro name xdl_free and evidence of calls to vim_free indicates deallocating/releasing caller-provided pointer.

functions.name: xdl_realloc
functions.reason: Realloc updates/returns a (possibly moved) allocation via pointer; interface implies returning a new/updated owned buffer to caller.

functions.name: xdl_free_script
functions.reason: Takes a pointer to an existing object and frees it via xdl_free; ends lifetime of referenced resource owned by caller.

functions.name: free_index
functions.reason: Static helper that frees nested allocations referenced by the passed histindex pointer using xdl_free; releases caller-owned fields.

functions.name: XDL_ALLOC_ARRAY
functions.reason: Documentation says it allocates an array and returns NULL on failure; macro allocates and assigns to output pointer p.

cfr.name: XDL_ALLOC_ARRAY
cfr.documentation: Allocate an array of nr elements, returns NULL on failure

functions.name: XDL_CALLOC_ARRAY
functions.reason: Documentation says it allocates an array of zeroed elements and returns NULL on failure; allocates memory for p.

cfr.name: XDL_CALLOC_ARRAY
cfr.documentation: Allocate an array of nr zeroed out elements, returns NULL on failure

functions.name: XDL_ALLOC_GROW
functions.reason: Documentation says it grows the array, frees old p on failure, and updates p/alloc; operation acquires/maintains allocated memory for caller.

cfr.name: XDL_ALLOC_GROW
cfr.documentation: Ensure array p can accommodate at least nr elements, growing the array and updating alloc (which is the number of allocated elements) as necessary. Frees p and returns -1 on failure, returns 0 on success

functions.name: xdl_free_classifier
functions.reason: Void function taking an object pointer and directly calls project free routines (xdl_cha_free/xdl_free), indicating it releases memory referenced by the passed object.

functions.name: xdl_free_ctx
functions.reason: Releases an xdfile_t via a pointer parameter and calls xdl_free, consistent with deallocation of caller-owned fields/resources.

functions.name: xdl_free_env
functions.reason: Takes an xdfenv_t* and frees it by calling xdl_free_ctx, indicating destruction of the passed environment object’s owned memory without returning a new allocation.

functions.name: xdl_cha_free
functions.reason: Void free-like function for a chastore_t passed by pointer; calls xdl_free to deallocate the referenced resource.

functions.name: xdl_cha_alloc
functions.reason: Returns void* and is backed by allocation evidence (calls xdl_malloc), transferring ownership of newly allocated memory to the caller.
